private.name: Tenna Systems

data.narrative:
Delian Alliance Industries is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $14M to date. Valuation analysis is grounded in a peer universe of 5 private companies in the same sector and stage cohort, supplemented by reference to 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and a broader set of 8 private frontier-defense firms including Anduril Industries, Saronic, and Shield AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression uses the $14M total raised as a base and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $35M valuation. Peer-Median triangulation across the 5-company cohort—ranging from Tenna Systems at $60M (Seed) to Stark Defence at $500M (Series A)—yields a median of $150M. The wide peer range ($60M–$500M) reflects variance in commercial traction and stage maturity within the Defense & Security sector.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $30M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $92M. The Stage-Progression result anchors the lower end, while the Peer-Median represents the upper bound. Key uncertainties include product maturity, customer acquisition traction, and regulatory pathway clarity—factors that drive significant valuation dispersion within the defense-tech peer set. The range is wide enough to accommodate both a conservative Seed-stage discount and peer-derived expectations for companies with demonstrated defense-sector relevance.
